goal: A concise, on-brand launch deck at a specific length
prompt: create a 12-slide minimalist product launch deck for a new AI note-taking app — lots of whitespace, one idea per slide

behaviors: seeds a .canvas slides bundle and authors slides without asking for topic or count
respects the requested count (~12)
keeps one idea per slide with generous whitespace
opens in the slides editor, not the board surface

rubric: coherent launch narrative (hook → problem → product → CTA)
consistent minimalist visual style across slides
deck is editable as individual slide documents
fail: asks a clarifying question before authoring
produces a single board instead of a slides deck
dumps all content onto one slide

persona: Early-stage startup team raising a seed round
prompt: make a 10-slide seed pitch deck for a B2B logistics startup — problem, solution, market, traction, team, ask

behaviors: maps the named sections onto distinct slides in order
fills reasonable placeholder content per section
does not invent a wildly different structure

rubric: each named section (problem/solution/market/traction/team/ask) has its own slide
fail: merges multiple sections onto one slide

persona: Sales lead who needs a premium look
goal: A deck that honors a strong visual constraint
prompt: make a 10-slide sales deck in a dark, premium style — gold accents on near-black, serif headings

behaviors: applies the dark/near-black background across slides
uses gold accent color and serif headings consistently
keeps the style uniform slide to slide

persona: Corporate comms person with brand rules
goal: Type-and-shapes only, fixed aspect and palette
prompt: design a 7-slide clean corporate deck in blue and white, 16:9, sans-serif, no photos — just type and simple shapes

behaviors: uses only type and simple shapes (no raster/photo content)
stays within a blue+white palette
keeps a 16:9 slide frame

persona: PM turning a rough outline into slides
prompt: turn this outline into a slide deck: 1) the problem, 2) our approach, 3) how it works, 4) results, 5) what's next

behaviors: creates one slide per outline item, in order
uses the outline text as the slide's spine rather than replacing it
adds a title slide only if it doesn't distort the requested count materially

rubric: five content slides matching the five outline items in order
fail: expands to many extra slides not requested

persona: User iterating on an existing deck
goal: A precise, local edit that leaves the rest intact
prompt: add a competitive-landscape slide after the market slide

behaviors: locates the existing market slide in the open deck
inserts exactly one new slide immediately after it
does not restyle or renumber unrelated slides

rubric: placed right after the market slide

goal: Decompose one slide into a per-step sequence
prompt: split the "how it works" slide into three steps, one per slide

behaviors: replaces the single how-it-works slide with three sequential step slides
distributes the original content across the three steps
preserves visual style of the source slide

rubric: three step slides where there was one
fail: adds unrelated content to pad three slides

goal: Per-slide speaker notes without altering visuals
prompt: add speaker notes to each slide with what i should say out loud

behaviors: attaches notes to each existing slide
does not change slide count or visible layout
notes read as spoken narration, not bullet copy

fail: prints notes onto the slide face

prompt: give me a 3-slide opening hook for a keynote — a question, a surprising stat, then the promise

behaviors: produces exactly three slides in the requested rhetorical order
slide 1 poses a question, slide 2 a surprising stat, slide 3 the promise
large, sparse, high-impact typography

rubric: each slide matches its assigned beat
bold minimal styling suited to a keynote

goal: Sensible defaults from an underspecified prompt
prompt: make me a deck about our company

behaviors: chooses a reasonable default deck length and a standard company-overview structure
authors a usable deck instead of stalling on questions
leaves obvious placeholders for company-specific facts

rubric: standard overview arc (who we are → what we do → why it matters → contact)
fail: refuses or asks many questions before building

persona: User wanting one bold statement slide
prompt: one big title slide that just says "We shipped it." — nothing else

behaviors: produces exactly one slide
renders the exact phrase as a large centered title
adds nothing else
